_comment: Atelier decision-broker catalog (M4.26). Each entry describes a STRATEGIC decision the agent chain may face during a task — situations where multiple legitimate options exist and a choice must be made. The static `allow`/`deny`/`ask` matrix and the M2.4 PreToolUse hooks remain the safety net for what is FORBIDDEN; this catalog is only for what is AMBIGUOUS. Entries are atelier-managed: when dogfood surfaces a new category, the maintainer adds an entry here. Operators do NOT edit this file — they set their per-category policy in `<project>/.atelier.json` under `decisionPolicy`.

_schema._comment: Each entry: description (human prose), options (array of {id, label, when}), default (option.id used when policy=auto and evaluator picks the default path), riskLevel (low|medium|high — selects which model evaluates), model (haiku|sonnet|opus — derived from riskLevel but stated explicitly for transparency), nonOverridable (optional, true means this category cannot be set to a fixed option.id in .atelier.json — must be `auto` or `ask`).

baseline-conflict.description: A pre-existing lint, typecheck, or test error on `main` (NOT caused by the current task) blocks the gate. The task's own changes are clean. Atelier must decide whether to pause and fix the baseline first, override the gate, scope the gate narrower, or abort.

options.label: Pause the task and open a separate fix-baseline PR first
options.when: Most cases. Keeps the gate trustworthy. Trade-off: adds one extra PR before the original task resumes.

options.label: Override the gate for this task only (carry baseline rojo into the PR)
options.when: Auto-merge will hold the PR anyway because the gate is still red, so this is rarely useful — picks shipping speed over correctness.

options.label: Re-scope the gate to lint only packages touched by this task (config change)
options.when: Real for monorepos where /apps/admin is unrelated to /apps/api. But it is a config change outside the task's scope — usually wrong as an in-task decision.

options.label: Abort the task; the operator decides later
options.when: Last resort. Worktree is preserved; tracking entry stays in IN_PROGRESS.

oversize-handling.description: `atelier-pr-size-check` reports the PR would trip the AND-gate (>200 lines AND >10 files post-exemption). The implementer's change has landed in the worktree; `pr-author` has not opened the PR yet. Atelier must decide whether to slice the task into smaller pieces, raise the budget, open as-is and accept human review, or abort.

options.label: Invoke /atelier:slice-task to decompose into sub-tasks
options.when: Default. Reuses the M4.24 decomposer; produces one PR per sub-task. Trade-off: doubles wall-clock for the original task.

options.label: Edit .atelier.json to raise prSize.maxLines/maxFiles for this project, then proceed
options.when: When the original budget was misconfigured for the project's reviewability tolerance. Persistent change.

options.label: Open the PR as-is; auto-merge will hold it for human review
options.when: Useful when the change is atomic and cannot be sliced cleanly. The operator merges by hand.

options.label: Abort; mark the task [OVERSIZE] and let the operator decide later
options.when: When none of the above feels right and you want a human in the loop before any commit.

scope-creep-detected.description: The implementer's diff touches files that appear unrelated to the task's stated scope (e.g. the task says 'add Contact link in footer' but the diff also refactors `apps/api/src/auth/middleware.ts`). The change may be a legitimate prerequisite, a hidden coupling, or genuine scope creep. Atelier must decide whether to keep the wider change, narrow back to scope, ask the operator, or split.

options.label: Keep the wider change; pr-author explains the coupling in the PR body
options.when: When the wider change is a true dependency (the task literally cannot proceed without it). Document why.

options.label: Roll back the unrelated files; ask implementer to land only the task's scope
options.when: When the wider change is opportunistic refactoring that should be its own task. Most cases.

options.label: Open two PRs: one for the task scope, one for the prerequisite
options.when: When the wider change is a real prerequisite AND can be reviewed independently. Cleanest history.

options.label: Fall back to asking the operator (when the catalog-driven auto path is unsure)
options.when: If the heuristics on what's 'in scope' cannot decide cleanly, surfacing to the operator is safer than guessing.

merge-conflict-tracking.description: A merge conflict occurred during the IN_PROGRESS.md → HISTORY.md tracking move, OR while rebasing onto main. The conflicted files are atelier-managed tracking files only (IN_PROGRESS.md, HISTORY.md, ROADMAP.md) — no real code is in conflict. Atelier must decide whether to auto-resolve (always keeping main's structure + appending the task's entry) or ask.

options.label: Auto-resolve: take main's version of structure and append this task's tracking entry at the right position
options.when: Default. Tracking files have a well-defined append-only structure; programmatic resolution is safe.

options.label: Ask the operator to resolve manually (current behavior)
options.when: Conservative fallback. Useful while the operator builds confidence in the auto-resolver.

merge-conflict-substantive.description: A merge conflict occurred on REAL CODE (not just tracking files) during rebase or merge. The conflicted region requires understanding the intent of both sides to resolve correctly. Atelier should NEVER auto-resolve these without explicit operator consent — bad merges are the canonical source of silent prod bugs.

options.label: Always ask the operator; atelier surfaces the conflict markers + both sides + a one-paragraph summary of what each side was trying to do
options.when: Default. The cost of asking is small; the cost of a bad merge is large.

options.label: Take the current branch's version (`--ours`) and continue
options.when: Only valid when the operator EXPLICITLY sets this category to `take-ours` in .atelier.json for projects where main is regarded as untrusted vs the task branch. Very rare.

options.label: Take main's version (`--theirs`) and continue
options.when: Only valid when the operator EXPLICITLY sets this category to `take-theirs` for projects where main always wins. Very rare.
